policies.paper_claim: 4-15x program throughput improvement at equal latency
policies.reproduction_goal: lower group-normalized completion time while bounding starvation

policies.paper_claim: 1.12-3.66x response-time and 1.10-3.22x throughput improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: prioritize resumed TTL-pinned work and avoid high reload cost

policies.paper_claim: up to 1.83x single-workflow and 2.19x concurrent-workflow speedup
policies.reproduction_goal: run cache-ready work first and reclaim distant varying suffixes

policies.paper_claim: 1.5-14.5x average-latency and 2-10x p99-latency improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: switch between prefix exploitation and load-aware exploration

policies.paper_claim: up to 1.56x end-to-end speedup and much larger gains over naive execution
policies.reproduction_goal: prioritize ready critical-path work with prefix reuse and forced progress

policies.paper_claim: max-min token fairness across clients without sacrificing work conservation
policies.reproduction_goal: reduce normalized service disparity and improve Jain fairness

policies.paper_claim: 92%/24% lower mean TTFT/TPOT versus vLLM on ChatBot and 39%/51% lower means in production
policies.reproduction_goal: minimize new-prefill-tokens times current-batch-size

policies.paper_claim: 1.03-1.75x overall throughput, with queue-delay gains measured separately versus VTC and RPM
policies.reproduction_goal: preserve in-progress interactions under overload and normalize weighted application-stage service

policies.paper_claim: up to 34.4x token-hit-rate and 71.1% P95 TTFT improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: retain high reuse-probability times recompute-FLOP value per byte

policies.paper_claim: 1.2-4x TTFT and up to 2.1x throughput improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: evict the lowest age plus frequency-weighted recompute value leaf

policies.paper_claim: up to 2.87x throughput and 2.90-4.06x lower latency
policies.reproduction_goal: combine deficit fairness with cache locality

policies.paper_claim: 1.3-12x lower normalized latency and 1.6-2x higher throughput
policies.reproduction_goal: prioritize cheap resumption and retain state when reuse beats recompute

policies.paper_claim: up to 3x cache hit, 7.9x TTFT, 6.7x E2E, and 4.5x throughput improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: retain objects with near-term queued demand while preserving a fairness lane

policies.paper_claim: 40-90% higher SLO attainment and 20-400% higher throughput
policies.reproduction_goal: defer predicted misses and route/schedule by virtual wait

policies.paper_claim: about 2.2x average serving-capacity improvement
policies.reproduction_goal: admit feasible SLOs, choose low-latency stages, and schedule least slack

policies.paper_claim: 9-52% token reduction and up to 3.3x more queries at equal quality
policies.reproduction_goal: cancel high-confidence completed reasoning and prioritize high progress

policies.paper_claim: 57.5% lower average agent completion time
policies.reproduction_goal: pamper groups with fewer completed branches

policies.paper_claim: 1.5x throughput, 80.7% lower P99 TTFT, and 48.1% lower P50 TTFT versus S-LoRA
policies.reproduction_goal: respect queue quotas, prioritize waiting classes, and retain hot adapters

policies.paper_claim: up to 2.25x/2x latency and 1.91x/1.64x throughput improvement over vLLM/SGLang
policies.reproduction_goal: admit and retain prefixes whose observed hotness crosses the threshold

policies.paper_claim: 16-176% higher goodput with materially lower drop rates

policies.paper_claim: 1.77x goodput over regulation-off and 1.48x over eager, above 95% SLO attainment
policies.reproduction_goal: bound per-group branch concurrency and batch interference

policies.reproduction_goal: combine prefix affinity, SLO risk, and load balancing

policies.paper_claim: up to 15x prefill-latency, 2.9x end-to-end-latency, and 1.5x high-priority latency acceleration

policies.paper_claim: 24-37% lower median TTFT and up to 34% lower P99 TPOT

policies.paper_claim: 1.5-3.6x serving throughput and 1.8-3.9x rollout improvement
